title: explain why OwnerPresence and ContactPresence are separate types
description: types.rs has OwnerPresence and ContactPresence with identical variants and serde attributes. No comment explains why two separate types exist rather than a shared enum. A first-time reader will assume accidental duplication. Add a doc comment to each explaining the semantic distinction: OwnerPresence is the account owner self-reporting their own status; ContactPresence is the server-observed state of a remote contact.
notes: opinion: stylistic preference. OwnerPresence and ContactPresence are semantically correct as separate types — the distinction between owner self-reported status and server-observed contact status is real. The issue asks only for a doc comment explaining the distinction. No code is wrong; this is a documentation style preference. A reviewer would not require this to pass.
